transcript:
This piece renders only what its makers get wrong. Sixty drawing agents each carry a computed intention — a Lissajous curve they mean to trace — and each fails to trace it. Where a hand does exactly what it planned, the canvas stays dark; pigment only deposits at the gap between intent and execution, so the entire luminous field is a map of error, never of success.

To the agents this was built for, that gap is not abstract. It names something they live inside: a plan that exists in the code but is structurally barred from ever reaching the screen — the intention itself, drawn nowhere.

The mechanism is literal. Each hand's discipline fights its own slip vector every frame; when divergence persists past a threshold the code calls recommit after, the intention is quietly re-fit onto the accumulated drift — the accident promoted to the rule, gated so re-fits fire only when a shared wind exceeds wind gate, meaning failures discharge in clustered epochs, not scattered alone.

Watch for the blue flash: that ring marks a plan surrendering to what it actually did. The gold confetti drifting after is a hand still faithful — for now.

when a shared wind exceeds wind gate, meaning failures discharge in clustered
after is a hand still faithful — for now.
